payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Cool. So that's awesome. Next announcement, for those who haven't had a chance to see it. A little over a year ago I Kickstarted a web series with a bunch of other really talented people. Sean Becker, who is director of The Guild, directed it. It's called Muzzled the Musical. Ashly Burch, a bunch of great talented people from The Lizzie Bennet Diaries and a bunch of other web series, Juliet Landau is in it. It's a great fun web series and it went live last week right before Halloween. So we have our three-episode arc up on YouTube. If you like musicals and you like fun silly dark, Doctor Horrible-style performances, then you're going to like this. Check it out, see if you like it. Let's see. Oh yeah, tomorrow is BlizzCon. BlizzCon, for all you crazy guys who are going or those who are watching on the digital online pass. A bunch of us Critters will be wandering the halls, doing panels and all kinds of fun stuff. Like, for me personally, I'll be there tomorrow at 4:30. I think, Liam, you are on that as well?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make more stuff for you guys, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the more that we can show the people upstairs that people do like our stuff and our content, the more you will get. So please go check it out if you have not yet. It helps.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Here, actually, do this with me. Let's make a double rainbow gif, for later. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's beautiful.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's on you, CritRoleSource.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's on you, internet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're welcome.

payload.raw_text: The last announcement is that they announced that The Witcher is coming to theaters in a couple of years, and that has nothing to do with us, other than the fact that I'm probably going to be at full mast for the next week, thinking of that possibility.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'm not familiar with that. What is that?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's some game. Takes place in outer space.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Is that a match three? Like Bejeweled?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's like Candy Crush, but with more death.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The last announcement is that they announced that The Witcher is coming to theaters in a couple of years, and that has nothing to do with us, other than the fact that I'm probably going to be at full mast for the next week, thinking of that possibility.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I directed one, too, called Yoyotoki Happy Ears. So that's on there too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: How's that go, again?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yoyotoki Happy Ears. It's really weird and funny, and it's actually really awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Also, Halo 5 is out this week, which Laura and I are in. It's amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not familiar with that. What is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's some game. Takes place in outer space.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that a match three? Like Bejeweled?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's like Candy Crush, but with more death.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's available on Android.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So that's pretty cool. So to get you guys up to speed with the circumstance that has happened, those especially that are tuning in for the first time. A couple known as the Briarwoods that took over the city of Whitestone five years before, murdering most of the ruling family of Percy-- other than himself, escaping-- have recently been doing political discussions with the home city of Emon. Vox Machina, our troop of intrepid adventurers, upon discovering this, battled them at Emon. They fled to Whitestone, and the party has then gone after to seek to destroy them and free the town, the once-home to Percival. In the process, it appears that there have been a number of warnings set, and the entire town has been long held under this dark, ominous rule that they have kept at bay. Apparently, they have found notes of previous, failed rebellions, and things that die here don't seem to stay dead. A number of zombified giants are wandering the city as these giant lurching sentinels. And apparently, a number of the cutthroats that helped the Briarwoods come to power in this town have now been given titles and land here and are now called the new nobles by the locals. During this process, you guys managed to sneak into the town, began setting out word of coming rebellion and that Percival de Rolo has returned and the Briarwoods' time is numbered. Upon meeting the previous chancellor to your father of the town, Archibald, you guys came to an understanding, and he was going to reach out to all of his contacts and let them know that the rebellion is happening. You guys then set out to set a two-pronged attack against two of the remaining new nobles in the town. Most of the party going to seek the outside of Count-- what's his name?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: They will. It'll be Mark Wahlberg.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, no, no!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Channing Tatum.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Will Ferrell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I will... I would not!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Channing Tatum is Geralt of Rivia.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Done! Let's make this happen.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Don't. Guys, I will stop this show. Your DM is out! If Channing Tatum becomes Geralt, I will be so furious. He's great at certain roles. I loved him in... I absolutely adored him in the 21 Jump Street movies.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I guess he's not coming on our show, jeez.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's enough of our silly talk. Let's go ahead and dive into the chaos that is the current circumstance. Keep in mind, the culmination of leading into Halloween with this story arc and now going and starting a rebellion in Whitestone on the Fifth of November has been a very delightful, unintentional coordination here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I almost wore my special spider belt, too, but it felt too weird.

payload.raw_text: So that's your triceratops form. After becoming a triceratops, you squashed the front guard that was there, you slammed through the front of the building, destroying the front door, and we cut to the other group who is stealthing through the rain. At which point, the okay was given to Grog, whether intentionally or not. Grog then charged to the front door of Tylieri's estate, and the rest of you prepared for impact. And that was where we left off, in the center of this chaos.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Duke Vedmeyer was the one that Scanlan went to, and then Tylieri is the one that the rest of you wandered outside. Now, as the rain pours down into the city, midday. Scanlan decided to open from a Polymorph spell, as a fly, to the top of the front doorstep of Vedmeyer's home. Then, proceeding to un-Polymorph and re-Polymorph into a triceratops. Of which, I have your statistics here for your triceratops form, if you want to come get that.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: In fact, roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Lovely decor.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You also notice that the entire interior is currently filled with guards. All seemingly prepared and almost expecting your arrival.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I think I can take them, guys. I think I can take them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You also see, at the top, Duke Vedmeyer himself. A large, hairless, grey-skinned goliath, wielding a giant two-handed sword, armored with a breastplate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Vecmeyer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Vedmeyer.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's a goliath?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's a goliath, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Vedmeyer is a goliath?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wow, that's going to be fun later, but not right now. Okay, do I know? So, let's assess my knowledge real quick. I know about him a little bit. He is one of the thugs that came into the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He is not a magic-wielder, as far as I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As far as you know, no. And at first glance, based on the fact that he is wearing heavy breastplate and is wielding a giant two-handed sword, you get the feeling he is probably not as heavily inclined towards the arcane arts as others you have encountered.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As I quickly survey the room, do I see anyone else who is a threat greater than a heavily armed guard? Anything that is a creepy wizard or a weird old mage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Currently, no. Currently, all you see are a series of Whitestone guards in uniform armor, all with heavy crossbows, currently nocked. And as you come blasting through the door, they are already arranged in what appears to be a waiting pattern, should anything try and charge its way into the house.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So they'll probably all shoot me, won't they?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's a goliath, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Vedmeyer is a goliath?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wow, that's going to be fun later, but not right now. Okay, do I know? So, let's assess my knowledge real quick. I know about him a little bit. He is one of the thugs that came into the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He is not a magic-wielder, as far as I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As far as you know, no. And at first glance, based on the fact that he is wearing heavy breastplate and is wielding a giant two-handed sword, you get the feeling he is probably not as heavily inclined towards the arcane arts as others you have encountered.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As I quickly survey the room, do I see anyone else who is a threat greater than a heavily armed guard? Anything that is a creepy wizard or a weird old mage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Currently, no. Currently, all you see are a series of Whitestone guards in uniform armor, all with heavy crossbows, currently nocked. And as you come blasting through the door, they are already arranged in what appears to be a waiting pattern, should anything try and charge its way into the house.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So they'll probably all shoot me, won't they?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In fact, roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So directly in front of me, there's a staircase going up?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Any other doors or exits?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are doors on each side that are currently locked, and other than that, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Those are locked doors?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They are closed currently, and they appear to be-- There's a giant wooden beam crossed over the front of each. Then there's the outside which leads to the exterior, to the raining outside of the household, and the stairs that go up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, man.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's now your worst nemesis.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Over here? It's about 35 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to turn right and blast through that door. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, it could be trapped. Well, we'll find out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Expect the unexpected with Scanlan Shorthalt.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He goes blasting through the doors.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I have to roll to blast through the doors?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We are going to find out. Yeah, go ahead and roll a strength check. Roll a d20 and add your strength modifier of the triceratops form.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, strength modifier says plus five. So that's only a 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: For a door!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me and doors don't get along too well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You, running, slam your head in, through, the horns protruding and tearing through the wood. The doors break inward, but the doors don't fully blast inward.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The door beat me!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The door is in bad shape. It won't take much more power to blast through.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: These fucking doors.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: From their direction it's kind of hard. Especially because there's a table and chairs up against it, and part of it has been crushed by a triceratops that was previously here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, one more round. What do you say, guys?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're not here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: One more round. I am going to-- fuck. Do I have line-of-sight on the door?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The door is right here. So you're by the window. So if you wanted to move over here, then you would have line of sight on pretty much everybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that would be so fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You could move and then move back. I'm only helping with gameplay strategy.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He could brain me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He could.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He could brain me in one shot. Okay. I will duck under the table so they think that's where I went, and then Dimension Door to the roof.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay! You duck under the table. I'm going to say make a deception roll to try and successfully feint them.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can they see the top of his head behind the table?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. Easy enough to do, because it will take a round for anyone to recover from that, anyway, because you have to use your action both turns. So as you are waiting up there, you cast Invisibility on yourself and wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, I'm going under the table."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sentences you never expect to hear in your D&D game.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you grab your head. Oh no! Do a very elaborate dive under the table.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Here I go under the table!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A little strange dull thud of arcane energy as you bamf up to the ceiling.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He is reaching under the table, ladies and gentlemen.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know, what does this mean? Oh, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, is that your turn? You are now up on the rooftop. There's rain pouring down. You are immediately soaked from the heavy storm that's now coming into Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. It's just me up here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Just you up there, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I will go invisible and just hang and just see what happens.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: They don't burn off, but you feel they might be a little singed. You'd have to give it a few days to recover. And you hear screaming down inside the tunnel and both of them are currently dealing with the fact that they're on fire. So that's your movement, that's your bonus action. You still have an action.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll hoof it over there and just look around and punch myself in the gut, (belch).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As you reach over, you look down, and there is a ladder that leads up to the front, and there are two guards that are currently making their way up to the roof. They look up and see you, and their eyes go wide within their helmets, and as you release the flames, all you see is their faces go, "Ah!" Just engulfing the entire tunnel. Roll 4d6.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't have any d6.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go. They both fail their DC 13 dexterity saving throws because I can't roll tonight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 15? Okay. They both take 15 points of fire damage and the entirety of that space has now burst into flames. You pull back because the heat is intense, backdraft-style, as you're at the top.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do my eyebrows burn off?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: A short rest is about half an hour to 40 minutes of waiting, so you might want to find a place that isn't quite so intense to do this. You can try and sneak into one of the nearby homes and do it, maybe.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's so cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You just going to stay there and wait it out?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I think I'll wait it out if I'm in a relatively safe place, because I can't really do anything for you guys, anyway. You're on your own.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. We'll use that to hop over to the other party.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm taking a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's good! Yes.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So 61. So you take half that, which would be 31 points of piercing damage on you in one volley of crossbow bolts at you. (shoosh) You protected Trinket, and the rage did descend it a little bit. The pain, as all these bolts are now sticking out of your chest. You look like this front-quilled porcupine. You just take the pain and grin for a second. You have that iron-y taste of blood reach the back of your throat from the immediate impact and it just further intensifies your rage. I'm going to have everyone here roll initiative because you're all acting simultaneously at this point.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I go in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Grog, you push your way in, Trinket following behind you right there. Immediately, you see the upper banister, right over the railing also, similar to what Scanlan encountered, has guards completely within this area, at the ready, crossbows pulled. In wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, god. He just keeps adding them!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just 12 more.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is Tylieri. All waiting, crossbows at the ready. The doors slam open, you guys rush in. For a second, there's nothing immediately within your range, and you look up and see an entire array of guards all trained upon you and Trinket. A voice behind them goes, "Loose!" (fshh)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: At least Trinket's behind me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (chuckles) What's your armor class?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're going to tell us when we can do something in the back, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. This is just-- because of the speed everything goes, he gets there before anybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Just curious.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No worries. All right, that is-- ooh. That is 27 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That is 15 to hit? No. Third one, that is 16 to hit? Nope. That is a 21 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 26 to hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's a 22 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All the shitty guards were over at the other house.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 14 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.text: Then three more.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep, that hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No. Last one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And ten to hit. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Six crossbow bolts come at you. That's eight points of piercing damage. Don't halve them yet. We'll add them all up and then we'll halve that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.text: So, eight plus 13. 21. Plus eight again, 29. Plus another nine.

preceding_turns.text: 38, plus another 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So 49, and then another 12.

payload.raw_text: Yeah. Unless you want to use your action to yell out for Trinket to attack, in which case, you can give Trinket a round of combat.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm going to use my stone's endurance to get rid of that stank.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One of them, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Roll, plus my constitution. 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so we'll say you reduce one of the crits from 16. The first one, take 15 points off that because I think one of the crits was 15. We'll say you used it on that one. That's 39 points of piercing damage, reduced by half. That will put you at 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: 18? Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We'll say you take 18 points of piercing damage from those four non-crits.

preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.text: So yeah. That ends the guards' volley. That brings us to Vex. You're up.

preceding_turns.text: Yes. And Trinket. Both.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so I run in after Percy and my brother.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And Trinket--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Are you going to dash? Because you can move, you can double move, using your action to move, if you want.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I can?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It is now his turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm wearing my helmet, my shiny helmet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know. To look at you, reach up with the throat that he's grasped, and sink his fangs directly into your throat, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (angry noise) I have a little collar. Does that help at all?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not in this moment. You have a big neck. Very big neck, and a very tiny collar. (laughs) That will be a 20 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. So you take nine points of piercing damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is that halved?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That is halved. Yeah. So say five, round up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: However, you do take 14 points of necrotic damage. That is not reduced. But your maximum hit points are now reduced by 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Got it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you feel that same similar cold feeling as the life blood's being drained out of your body and you see his eyes cresting at you with this wild glare. That ends your turn, Vax?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's it.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Doing so gives you advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using strength during this turn. So yes, I apologize. It does give you advantage on all of your attacks. I misread that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't it cancel out after one roll? Advantage versus disadvantage is one roll, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It ended on the first attack, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It was a one on the first attack, yeah. It's until your next turn. Attacks against you get advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I thought his first bite was the first one.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, so all attacks, even follow-up attacks get advantage, even though he only gets advantage on the first one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll read it for you right now. It's a cool ability when you're not the only tank in the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it is not! It's the worst ability ever.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's a good ability if you're only fighting one person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but attack rolls against you have advantage until your next turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Because he's out of it. He's going cuckoo.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, did you not have-- Jesus, never use that thing again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm using it every time. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (quietly) Oh, shitfuck. (louder) That's okay. That's okay!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Stuff happens. Give him a bonus 15 HP or something.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Since you did not do reckless strike last time, they are not with advantage. That is 19?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no. I'll stay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that brings us to the guards' turn. They all reload their crossbows. These four are now going to make shots at you, Percy. These three are going to take shots-- all five of these guys are going to take shots at Grog now. They're trying to take you down so they can move onto the next target. So four shots on you, Percy. That is 20?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That does not hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you take from three heavy crossbow bolts at you. Seven, 19, 32 points of piercing damage. All three bolts stick into your chest. They're big, long, thick, heavy crossbow bolts. One of them gleans off your shoulder and catches the edge of the wall, but the other two have actually sunk into your torso and are sticking out of you. The other five against you, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Nice. All right, so (whack) the hammer comes streaking down, slamming into the guard there. His crossbow shatters from the impact and the flames begin to lick up the front of his body. The second slam smacks him again. This time, you hear the armor ring out from the metal-on-metal impact. He kind of shakes from it. The third time you're just in the middle of this series of swinging and he backs up, and you end up smashing the banister instead of him, the hammer cracking part of the ground on the second floor. He looks pretty messed up from that series of attacks. All right, that ends your turn, Grog. It's now the guards' turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm up?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, I can't get through the-- I mean, I don't know what the fuck--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You can get through the Wind Wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What am I going to do to gas? Hit it? Can I start running up the motherfucking stairs and jump up like Vax did? He said to kill!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, yeah. You can get up to there, and you're just within reach to get one guy over the banister, probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! I'd like to do that. That's a 23.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That hits. More attacks?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Oh, how many attacks do I get?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All of them. That was just your movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yay, I get all of them?! That's amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your barbarian 40 feet of crazy movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yay! 22!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shit! 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12 does not hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) The first two hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: 13. 17 for the first one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, 17 damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: 15, and 17 again. Two plus two.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You are flanking him, though. You have advantage on the attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I move the mist to the next guy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. You use a bonus action to re-curse him, by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I had a bonus action, so I just used it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's right, you do. Technically, you used the bonus action this turn to put it onto him from the last turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Did I? You're technically correct, so go ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're probably going to do it on your next turn, anyway, so we'll just leave it there. It's fine. But keep that in mind, as this is a new ability.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (sneezes)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Gesundheit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Bless you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Vax, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that fellow who's enveloped in schmutz right next to Grog?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm going to stab him. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Your answer for everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: First one is not that great. It's a 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 14 misses.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Second one is a 26.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That ends your turn. It is now the guards' turn. They all...

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (groans) He falls over (thudding) down the stairs, past you, Percy. You let him tumble by as he slides to a stop at the very bottom of the stairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I put my foot on him to stop him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, Vex stops him in place, face-down. There's a smear of his blood across the steps. This once-nice, fine wood is now completely coated in crimson. (laughs) Okay. Grog, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it me? (stuttering) Is it just a row of guys in front of me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I would like to hold my turn. Because if I stand in front of them, nobody can do anything to them.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You can.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's nice of you. You could just go (whoosh) and cross them off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How many's left?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are five nervous-looking guards right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They were trying to haul ass out?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nah, I'll hold. Whoever's next can go.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So polite.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That is really kind of you, Grog.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll give you advantage on this because you're raging and it's reckless and it's just funnier.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That definitely hits him, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 27 hits. Go ahead and roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 11 points of damage. Across that guy. Okay. Trinket reaches out and strikes across one of the guys, the guy you Hunter's Marked, who takes the claw across the side of his head, and you hear him yell out in pain, (scream) as he makes it out into the rain. Hurt, but not taken down. He continues to run past, and they all dart outside into the rainstorm.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll take my turn now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Okay, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I leap over the banister with my war hammer high and jump down on the guy that's closest to me, bringing it down with a reckless attack and great weapon master.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make an athletics check for the jump.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh no, don't fall on your ass.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that's true. 13.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Oh, that's right! You can do it for up to a minute. Good call.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And I say, "Get inside now."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Next up is Keyleth. Are you going to do anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do I kill him or do we keep him?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You have to make that decision yourself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is he coming back inside?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's standing there now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's currently standing about ten feet outside in the rain, just (shaky breathing).

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is that a window I'm up against, behind me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Actually, no. There is no window to the front here. It's just a wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm just going to go, "Excuse me. Excuse me, Trinket. Sorry." And I'm going to peek around the door and go (shkoof) and Sunbeam him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're going to Sunbeam?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That guy. That scared-shitless guy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Okay. What level is Sunbeam?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's what I just did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What level is Sunbeam?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: How many 6th-level spells per day can you cast?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's a concentration spell.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. The two of you take a while upstairs looking about the rooms. You manage to acquire from just some of the local bits of cabinets and dust, you gather about 230 gold pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You begin to take the plume of smoke and filter into the dark rain clouds and you make the smoke go to a thicker, thicker black and take the next few moments to begin sculpting in the air until eventually we have a very well-defined de Rolo crest now staring down at the entire city of Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Something's happening! Something's happening in the sky!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I think it's a magic wielder of some sort. Something-- I must flee! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Investigation checks from both of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did I find anything on Smash-Face?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) That's what you rolled the last time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've got to put this one away for a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What did you roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I rolled a nine.

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the alcove
payload.raw_text: Correct. All right. You make your way to the alcove. As you're darting through, you can see in the distance small pocketed sounds of yells and the occasional sound of some sort of large-scale crumbling or crashing of wood and stone. Occasionally, darting past the streets, you see militant occupants of Whitestone: armored, rushing through, shouting orders to each other, some coming back wounded and helping their friends back. The midday rebellion has definitely started, and you get the sense that the initial surprise has been effective, but the longer this runs, the longer this wears on, the more and more of a body count this will be.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. So making up for the bounding chaos that is Grog throughout the city, you manage to still make your way largely as a group stealthed to-- are you heading to the alcove, you said?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's where we had our little crash-base set up, right?

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the front
payload.raw_text: You have not, but you all meet up back at the alcove, communicating over your earpieces. You make your way to the front of the building. The doors are closed and locked.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Seeing the chaos, can I get the Dust of Tracelessness and start trying to sprinkle it behind us?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Am I with these guys yet?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Right now, I'd say you're close to three in the afternoon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We may have just started a revolution.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does he look familiar to you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You don't recognize us, it's not safe to walk these streets for outsiders, so we were using a little bit of trickery. This is us. We're the same people you were dealing with before.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Vox Machina.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We just need to rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay. You can rest here, but how long?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Until we're healed, and then we'll go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Overnight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like we should get word out to somebody, though, because--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To what? To hold the revolution?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like everybody's going to die before we even get to the castle!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's the thing. I don't know if this is the time to rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But we don't have any spells.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm okay. We can maybe make do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I can't make do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You might be cool, but we're fucked.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: How about we stay here until 4 00, 5 00 in the morning--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time is it now?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, you guys go ahead, use your hit dice to heal up while you're taking your short rest for those who've taken damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's what I'm talking about. They're vampires.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, they're zombies. They're undead.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're zombies.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They're undead.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Those are two different things, huh?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They are two very different things.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Grog learns. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So what's the plan?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We short rest and get back out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We take a short rest and then exterminate.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, join the rebellion or ride around town telling everyone, "quell?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're taking down these damn giants. These giants are killing people left and right; we watched them crush people on our way here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We tell them that they take cover during the night.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to roll four hit dice.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well, you guys are taking a short rest, about 30 minutes to an hour, so you have about two and a half, three hours before it goes down entirely.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, dear god almighty. Okay. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Percy, Percy, Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't mean to be doing this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's great. Just glad you didn't take the Alertness feat. Then things would have gotten stupid.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are you healing Trinket?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How much more does he need?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's at 36. He needs 24 more.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Actually, I'm going to be using the Hat of Disguise just because it's probably safe. Does anybody want a Headband of Initiative?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Do you not need it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can't wear two things on my head.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't have anything on my head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So you've got plus two to your initiative, and I'm losing plus two to my initiative. It's called a Headband of Initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You already have an eight to your initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's very mystical.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How much time do we have before the sun that is being blocked by the rainclouds goes down?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It doesn't matter. It still manages to keep its zombie form, though it seems like every single hit you're taking chunks off its body, but it continues to push through the damage until eventually it swings at you with the hit, Bad News goes off as it blasts through the center of its torso and takes out the back of its spine. It can't keep its physical form together, and as its body lunges forward, you throw both of your daggers, which go (thoonk thoonk) into each of its eyes. It falls forward. The torso seems to roll on impact and come after you like a slow-moving boulder for a few feet, but since you're all moving forward, you manage to outrun it, just barely keeping out of the way. The legs walk on their own for two more steps before it just falls forward into the mud, relieving all its intestines into the rain-soaked ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What did you get?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 27 and an 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Both hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I got 28.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. And against Percy, that is a 17 and a 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: 17 misses. The 20 hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You take 16 points of bludgeoning damage as its fist comes down and (whack) slams into your side just as Bad News fires off and your daggers go (dagger sounds) towards it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Keen Dagger, Flametongue, combined 20 hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Good to know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And as soon as I'm able, I'm backing up and bringing down another lightning bolt.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I've got another attack. That was just one attack that I did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You need your spells. We need to hide. We need to run.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's okay, this is a concentration spell. I can keep doing it. I'm not blowing spells on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: 22 points of damage, plus he's knocked prone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Easy enough. Through the daggers--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he can make a con saving throw, though.
